mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving real output and earnings breadth, causing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is evaluated continuously.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth does not produce higher subsequent 20-day SPX returns than lower growth states in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase of more than 50 basis points over 6 months raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Joint payroll acceleration and falling unemployment identify broad labor-income expansion, which raises expected household spending and corporate revenues, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 300 thousand and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-strength state fails to outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return in walk-forward tests or if either single-series effect fully explains the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices without a commensurate rise in the federal funds rate signals an inflation-risk premium shock, compressing equity valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 6% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0.25pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-gap episodes do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts contraction greater than 10% over 3 months signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward testing or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly retail sales momentum signals near-term consumption strength and supports earnings expectations for cyclical sectors, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not produce higher average 5-day SPX returns than negative RSAFS momentum in walk-forward tests or if the directional accuracy is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and retail sales are both firm while the federal funds rate is rising, policy tightening dominates growth news and compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both exceed their 6-month moving averages and FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0.50pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strong-activity tightening state does not underperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns or if the FEDFUNDS interaction adds no negative explanatory power.
